topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The doorbell rang, a jarring intrusion into the quiet afternoon. I glanced at the peephole, my jaw tightening before I even moved. A package sat on the welcome mat—a bulky cardboard box, clearly addressed to the previous occupant. My knuckles were white as I gripped the doorframe. I didn't want it. I certainly didn't want anything from them. With a frustrated sigh that tasted like stale air, I wrestled the box inside, the sound of the tape ripping echoing loudly in the hallway. I immediately moved it to the darkest corner of the storage closet, pulling the door shut with unnecessary force. Every muscle in my body felt coiled, ready to spring, waiting for whatever unwelcome thing this delivery might contain to surface.

[story 2]

When the delivery person handed over the parcel, I didn't meet their eyes. My gaze was fixed on the label, scrutinizing the handwriting as if searching for some hidden threat. A knot formed deep in my stomach, a tight, unpleasant pressure that made my breath catch. "This isn't for me," I stated, my voice clipped and low, betraying none of the churning resentment inside. I took the package with two fingers, careful not to let my skin brush against the cardboard. The weight of it felt like an accusation. I shoved it toward the utility room, slamming the door shut behind it with a decisive thud. I spent the next hour pacing, my boots scuffing the linoleum, my jaw working silently, unable to settle until the object was safely out of sight.

[story 3]

The sound of the delivery truck pulling away left an oppressive silence in its wake. I stared at the package sitting on my porch, a silent, unwanted visitor. A slow burn started in my chest, a simmering irritation that made my hands clench into fists at my sides. I didn't want to acknowledge it, didn't want to engage with the ghost of the person who lived here before me. With a sharp exhale, I snatched the box up. My movements were jerky, propelled by a deep-seated refusal. I carried it across the threshold, my shoulders rigid, and practically threw it into the deepest recesses of the basement, the slam of the door sounding like a declaration of war against the intrusion.
